name: narrative_evolution
description: Track how the narrative around a topic or source has evolved over time. Clusters KB content into monthly periods and summarises the dominant stance, key claims, and tone for each period. Use for questions like: 'How has the Fed narrative shifted over 6 months?', 'Has [source] changed their view on [topic]?', 'What was the consensus on [topic] in early 2025 vs now?'. Returns a timeline of monthly summaries. Token-intensive — use on-demand only, not in loops.

topic.description: Free-text topic or question to track (e.g. 'Fed rate cuts', 'central bank policy shift', 'AI regulation').

timeframe.description: Lookback window. Options: '1m' (1 month), '3m' (3 months), '6m' (6 months, default), '1y' (1 year).

account.description: Optional: restrict to a single source account_id (e.g. 'jeff_snider'). Leave null to query all sources for the topic.

user_id.description: User isolation key. Pass from session context.

periods_found.description: Number of distinct calendar months found in the result set.

periods_analysed.description: Number of periods for which an LLM summary was generated.

timeline.description: Ordered list of monthly narrative summaries (ascending by date).

chunk_count.description: Total chunks found in this period.

analysed_count.description: Chunks used for the LLM summary (up to 5).

key_claims.description: Main claims made in this period.

tone.description: Overall tone: bullish | bearish | cautious | neutral | mixed.

error.description: Non-null if the query failed or no content was found. Inform the user and suggest they try a broader timeframe or check that content has been ingested for this topic.
